One man dead, one in custody following stabbing in St. Catharines   SAINT CATHARINES, Ont. — A 28-year-old man is facing a charge of second-degree murder following a stabbing in St. Catharines’ Old Pine Trail area Thursday night.

Second visitor in 3 days gored by bison at Yellowstone National Park   YELLOWSTONE NATIONAL PARK, Wyo. — For the second time in three days, a visitor to Yellowstone National Park was gored by a bison, park officials said Thursday.

Some Texas schools may call slavery 'involuntary relocation'   AUSTIN, Texas — Public schools in Texas would describe slavery to second graders as “involuntary relocation” under new social studies standards proposed to the state’s education board.

Family of Regis Korchinski-Paquet sues officers, watchdog two years after her death   Relatives of a Toronto woman who fell from her balcony while police were in her highrise apartment more than two years ago are hoping a newly-filed lawsuit will shed more light on her death, their lawyer said.

NHL notes: Avalanche's Stanley Cup parade a wild one   One day, the Maple Leafs and other Cup-cursed teams behind them hope to engage in the antics the Colorado Avalanche enjoyed Thursday.
Raptors lock up key free agents Chris Boucher, Thaddeus Young   The Toronto Raptors wasted little time taking care of their own in free agency on Monday.

D the key as Argos' beleaguered unit must step up against Bombers   From Royce Metchie’s vantage point as the Argos’ starting safety, the team’s secondary should be okay once it focuses on the game’s details.

Andreescu, Shapovalov both ousted from second round of Wimbledon   LONDON — Canadians Bianca Andreescu and Denis Shapovalov were both eliminated from Wimbledon after suffering second -round losses at the All England Tennis Club on Thursday.

Blue Bombers vs Argonauts Week 4 Picks and Predictions: Champs Too Much for Toronto   Week 4 in the CFL wraps up on Monday night with the Winnipeg Blue Bombers facing off against the Toronto Argonauts. The two-time defending Grey Cup champion Blue Bombers look to go 4-0 while the 1-1 Argos try to bounce back from an ugly road loss last week.

Rays vs Blue Jays Picks and Predictions: Tampa Takes Advantage of Kikuchi   The Toronto Blue Jays were unable to pull off the sweep of the Boston Red Sox, falling 6-5 in extra innings last night, but they’ll have to put that behind them quickly as they welcome another American League East foe to town in the Tampa Bay Rays.
Today’s MLB Prop Picks: Veteran First Basemen Ride High   The month of June concludes with a fairly light MLB slate, but we’ll try to make the most of it with a trio of player prop picks!
UFC 276 Volkanovski vs Holloway Picks and Predictions: Revenge to the Max   Alexander Volkanovski vs. Max Holloway 3 is the co-main event for a stacked card of UFC 276 betting on July 2.

UFC 276 Strickland vs Pereira Picks and Predictions: Strickland Makes a Statement   Sean Strickland vs. Alex Pereira is scheduled for three rounds in the middleweight division as a featured attraction of UFC 276 in Las Vegas. This unofficial title eliminator may determine who will get the first crack at the winner of Israel Adesanya vs. Jarod Cannonier.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

CRIME HUNTER: Times Square Killer linked to 1968 murder   During the day, Richard Cottingham would toil as a computer programmer in Manhattan, across the Hudson River from his suburban New Jersey home.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

Ex-wife of slain Microsoft exec and father of four allegedly wanted to ‘shut him up’   The death of a Microsoft executive remains unsolved but a surprising new development came out of a Florida tattoo parlour.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question

EDITORIAL: Federal services collapsing under Trudeau   Airports recently saw half of their expected flights pushed back or cancelled over a seven-day period due to government screw-ups. It’s a truly remarkable statistic.

LILLEY: Despite denials, Trudeau government policies still the problem with airport delays   Flights are being cancelled, luggage is piling up, custom halls are lined up and a blame game is being played.

GOLDSTEIN: Despite political rhetoric, the world’s going back to fossil fuels    Sensing now is not the time, the Trudeau government is delaying until next year a second carbon tax that will increase the cost of gasoline by an estimated five to 11 cents per litre by 2030.
OPINION: As doctors, we say Canada's federal COVID-19 policies need to go   By: Dr. Neil Rau, Dr. Martha Fulford, Dr. Pooya Kazemi and Dr. Ari Joffe
Poison frontman Bret Michaels hospitalized hours before show   Report says rocker may have had a bad reaction to medication related to COVID-19 as a result of his diabetes
Daughter of Charlie Sheen and Denise Richards escapes car crash   It's only been a few months since she passed her driving test

Bill Cosby tells how ‘murderers, rapists, bank robbers’ and ‘wife beaters’ applauded him leaving jail   Bill Cosby has told how he was applauded by “murderers, rapists, bank robbers” and “wife beaters” as he left jail.
Tim Allen not a fan of ‘Lightyear’: 'It has no relationship to Buzz'   Tim Allen has criticized the Toy Story spinoff Lightyear after he was replaced by Chris Evans .
R Kelly to face second trial in August on child sex abuse images charges   R Kelly will face a second trial in August.

Canada to fight poor diet with labels for sugar, salt, saturated fat   OTTAWA — Foods high in sugars, sodium and saturated fat will come with clear warning labels in Canada starting in 2026, the federal government said on Thursday, in an effort to promote healthier eating choices and reduce chronic health risks.

Rihanna’s Fenty Beauty out to prove Africa is viable market   Rihanna, whose full name is Robyn Rihanna Fenty, redefined the cosmetics industry when the line debuted in 2017 with 40 shades of foundation.

ASK AMY: Final break-up does not require details   Dear Amy: I’ve been in an on-and-off relationship with a man whose handsy, flirtatious style of interacting with women he finds attractive has always bothered me. I’ve broken up with him over this and have explained in detail several times how humiliating it feels to me when he acts this way.

SUNshine Girl Marijana   SUNshine Girl Marijana is back to spice it up! The Red Hot Chili Peppers fan enjoys travelling, shopping, swimming and watching movies, while her dream car is a white BMW. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Chantelle   Here’s another shot of SUNshine Girl Chantelle. Our 5-foot-5, blue-eyed Sagittarius, who is back from her 2019 shoot, is into bartending and dreams of being a model. (Toronto Sun)
SUNshine Girl Larissa   SUNshine Girl Larissa, our fit, 5-foot, brown-eyed Virgo, loves bodybuilding and spending time at the gym. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Nikki   SUNshine Girl Nikki is a dog groomer who likes hockey. Her favourite movie is "The Fast and the Furious" and her dream car is a 1970 Oldsmobile 442 W-30. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)
SUNshine Girl Emily   SUNshine Girl Emily loves yoga, hiking and writing in her journal. This Tesla-loving Taurus posed for us in May 2018. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun)

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.

Over half of people using medical cannabis to combat chronic pain had withdrawal symptoms: study   Clusters of symptoms occur after significant periods of not using marijuana.
Florida man's idea to hide two cannabis cigars in his shoe was a bust   Man arrested for weed possession and driving with a suspended licence.

Toronto-based company partners with Wiz Khalifa on line of psychedelic and functional mushrooms   While the psychedelic mushrooms will only be available in the Netherlands, functional home-grow kits are set to launch in the U.S., Europe and Canada.

Lawyer looking to secure cannabis retail spot for client tried to bribe police chief   The lawyer approached a close relative of the police chief and offered that person $32,250 to speak to the chief.
